entry.title: Group Reporting Validation Rules differents for different periods
entry.content:
I'm in a situation where some validation rules are only applicable during the year-end closing, while others are applicable during all the month-end closing.

How can I configure the validation rules to ensure that the rules that are not applicable are not taken into consideration during the month-end closing?

entry.title: Re: Single Persistent Table for Two Different Entities
entry.content:
I don't see any problem from the framework,

it's your problem to insert the same key to the table. you have to fix your coding.

entry.title: Re: Alternative Reconciliation Account for Customer
entry.content: hello, it is working... flexible posting had to be defined

entry.title: Re: Material document generated automatically from STO go...
entry.content:
Hello @Aicha_000 

Consider the notes:

  • 522591 - FAQ: Output determination in inventory management (IM)
  • 426554 - Output determination checklist for goods movements

to verify that your output determination settings are correct.

The 2nd screenshot shows that the "Print via output control" is not set. It can be preselected with NDR = X user parameter. It is explained in the note 3079393 - MIGO: How to set checkbox "Print via Output control" in header data by default

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: BusinessObject DEV CMS DB deleted .How to refresh it...
entry.content: cms.exe may be running but it's not functional. Check Windows event log and the CMS logs.

entry.title: Re: Payment run without postings
entry.content: Very nicely explained and good documentation, Very much appreciated, Keep it up

entry.title: Start lean with RAP (Restful ABAP programming model) #1
entry.content:
Restful ABAP Programming Model... I went through openSAP course, I bought SAP press books. But somehow RAP seemed a bit scary.  I am a developer who likes to understand what I am writing and why. Looking at examples of RAP, the number of keywords in behaviour definition and the implemenation classes with EML just seemed a bit overwhelming... Is it just me?

I would start with a bit of a context - I am originally ABAP developer who learnt UI5 and got heavily into fullstack development. I spent a lot of time building apps on ECC on HANA db. That means not yet S/4 (but close!), ABAP 7.5, for oData using mostly what I would call BOPF-less ABAP Programming Model for Fiori => SEGW with CDS views as referenced data source but not using BOPF cause at that version BOPF object can't be generated out of CDS. For implementing CRUD I would just add annotations below to my CDS exposed as referenced data source and then redefine methods for saving stuff in DPC_EXT class.

@ObjectModel.createEnabled: true

@ObjectModel.updateEnabled: true

@ObjectModel.deleteEnabled: true

With that and a few other pieces of ABAP when CDS wouldn't be enough or for things like changesets or stream processing I was able to built any complex applications efficiently fully utilizing the power of SADL and CDS while having full flexibility of ABAP when needed.

So this blog posts will be about achieving something similar in RAP in most simple and lean way I could find. Alright, let's start with my key recommendations. They might sound radical so please take it as a way to start with RAP, not as a best practice (though in my experience following approach can work very well for productive developments).

1) do unamanged behaviour - you will understand exactly what you are doing

2) do behaviour definition and implementation directly on ZC* (consumption) view - skip added complexity of behaviour projection layer, it's optional

3) trim behaviour definition to bare minimum that you clearly understand. Remove everything that you don't need or don't understand

4) don't use EML - everything can be done with traditional ABAP, EML is optional - understand first what you are doing with traditional ABAP, later add EML if suitable

Now here comes the example, let's assume simple model of 2 CDS views - for Purchase Order Header and Purchase Order Items. And app that allows displaying and creating purchase orders

 

define root view entity ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder as select from I_PurchaseOrder
composition [1..*] of ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem as _Items
{
    key PurchaseOrder,
    PurchasingGroup,
    PurchasingOrganization,
    Supplier,
    CompanyCode,
    DocumentCurrency,
    PaymentTerms,
    
    _Items
}
define view entity ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem as select from I_PurchaseOrderItem
association to parent ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder as _Header on $projection.PurchaseOrder = _Header.PurchaseOrder
{
    key PurchaseOrder,
    key PurchaseOrderItem,
    Plant,
    PurchaseOrderItemText,
    NetPriceAmount,
    OrderQuantity,
    DocumentCurrency,
    PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit,
    
    _Header
}

 

Let's continue with behaviour definition - following the recommendations nr.1 and 2 we generate unmanaged behaviour definition directly for ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder. (please note that unmanaged here stands for the CUD operations! querying data with GET requests will be still managed by SADL pulling from CDS views so no coding there). When you generate it, it will look like this:

 

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_c_leanrap_purchaseorder unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder //alias <alias_name>
//late numbering
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _Items { create; }
}

define behavior for ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem //alias <alias_name>
//late numbering
lock dependent by _Header
authorization dependent by _Header
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) PurchaseOrder;
  association _Header;
}

 

Now follow recommendation nr.3 and trim it... we want to create purchase order with items. Let's remove everything not directly related to that and we get to something like this:

 

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_c_leanrap_purchaseorder unique;

define behavior for ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder
{
  create;
  association _Items { create; }
}

define behavior for ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem

{
  create;
}

 

Finally let's get to behaviour implementation that follows recommendation nr.4 - no EML. In a nutshell, just 3 methods are implemented there - methods for create on header and item level will be called first and take data coming from frontend and save it into static attributes of transactional buffer class - lhc_buffer. And later method save will find it there in buffer and call BAPI for creating Purchase Order. As simple as that - no EML, no special RAP commands. Just search and look at all places where lhc_buffer is used. It's simplified example but actually it's implementing deep insert - header and items collected and saved with single BAPI call.

 

CLASS lhc_buffer DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-DATA: ss_bapi_po_header  TYPE bapimepoheader,
                ss_bapi_po_headerx TYPE bapimepoheaderx,
                st_bapi_po_item    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoitem,
                st_bapi_po_itemx   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoitemx.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder RESULT result.

    METHODS rba_Items FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys_rba FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder\_Items FULL result_requested RESULT result LINK association_links.

    METHODS cba_Items FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities_cba FOR CREATE ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder\_Items.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD create.

    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_header_create>).

      lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_header = VALUE #( doc_type     = 'ZPO'
                                               purch_org    = <ls_header_create>-PurchasingOrganization
                                               pur_group    = <ls_header_create>-PurchasingGroup
                                               vendor       = <ls_header_create>-Supplier
                                               comp_code    = <ls_header_create>-CompanyCode
                                               currency     = <ls_header_create>-DocumentCurrency
                                               pmnttrms     = <ls_header_create>-PaymentTerms ).

      lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_headerx = VALUE #( doc_type    = abap_true
                                                purch_org   = abap_true
                                                pur_group   = abap_true
                                                vendor      = abap_true
                                                comp_code   = abap_true
                                                currency    = abap_true
                                                pmnttrms    = abap_true ).

      EXIT. "we expect always 1 header to come
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD rba_Items.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cba_Items.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem RESULT result.

    METHODS rba_Header FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys_rba FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem\_Header FULL result_requested RESULT result LINK association_links.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD read.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD rba_Header.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_item_create>).

      APPEND VALUE bapimepoitem( po_item    = sy-tabix
                                item_cat    =  '0'
                                acctasscat  = 'C'
                                plant       = <ls_item_create>-Plant
                                short_text  = <ls_item_create>-PurchaseOrderItemText
                                quantity    = <ls_item_create>-OrderQuantity
                                po_unit     = <ls_item_create>-PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit
                                net_price   = <ls_item_create>-NetPriceAmount
                                matl_group  = 'TEST' )
                   TO lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_item.

      APPEND VALUE bapimepoitem( po_item    = sy-tabix
                               item_cat     =  abap_true
                               acctasscat   = abap_true
                               plant        = abap_true
                               short_text   = abap_true
                               quantity     = abap_true
                               po_unit      = abap_true
                               net_price    = abap_true
                               matl_group   = 'TEST' )
                  TO lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_itemx.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_ZC_LEANRAP_PURCHASEORDER DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

    METHODS finalize REDEFINITION.

    METHODS check_before_save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_ZC_LEANRAP_PURCHASEORDER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.

    DATA ls_new_po TYPE bapimepoheader.
    DATA lt_return TYPE bapiret2_t.

    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CREATE1'
      EXPORTING
        poheader  = lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_header
        poheaderx = lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_headerx
        testrun   = abap_false
      IMPORTING
        expheader = ls_new_po
      TABLES
        return    = lt_return
        poitem    = lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_item
        poitemx   = lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_itemx.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup_finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

In the next part(s) of this blog series we will take a look at adding more features while sticking to "lean RAP" recommendations:

- implementing error handling and late numbering

- updating both header and item fields for existing Purchase Orders (changesets)

- adding new items to existing Purchase Order with "create by association" operation

- implementing concurrency handling with etags

Hope this blog series will help a few more fellow developers to transition from SEGW and DPC_EXT classes to RAP ;-).

entry.title: Re: How to write a Groovy Script For Multilable, and what is the Extension do I use if using JavaScr
entry.content: I tried the below link however, its giving me the Script Error in CPI. hxxps://www.codeconvert.ai/javascript-to-groovy-converter

entry.title: Re: Where can I configure the “To-Do Task Reminder” email template from the Manager start page?
entry.content: The two new email templates in this release are ILXTrainingOverdue (which is triggered from the new ILX home page or Team view) and the ILXApprovalNotification (which is triggered by an APM). We adjusted the emails templates on each of these to meet our needs from a wording perspective. So while the manager themselves do not have the option to change the message, you can adjust the master template to your companies needs (perhaps adding that softer language).

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:

Scope Packages and Scope Request Entry Points are showing disabled by default :

Warm Regards,

RP

entry.title: Re: how to add multiple report in one report during run time
entry.content: Hi thanks for your reply, but i didint use ur solution but i found a different alternative solution , which i will post after testing it properly so that it will be helpful to others. Kindly wait for some time, thanks

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations on your S
entry.content:
Hello @hariprasadgud ,

We are still in the process of determining what the best process is for us exactly.

We want to follow your recommendation to not do any configuration change in production directly. So, I am looking for options to block that particular access for System Admins. To prevent making changes by mistake.

Do you have any best practices on role based permissions? For Manage Business Configuration I can easily change the access from Edit to View only. But for other parts like Company System and Logo Settings it is all or nothing. Ideally I would like to have that option for all manage and feature permissions. Do you have an example of a System Admin role who who should not make any changes but can view the relevant settings and configuration?

Or should we just tell everyone not to make changes and run change audit reports to keep on top of accidental changes and revert them so systems stay aligned?

Anything on this subject would help in our exploration so looking forward to your reply.

Thank you,
Roos🌹

entry.title: Re: issue with production liters
entry.content:
You should provide the exact error message (there are some online documentation and notes for some similar messages

  • Examples: Error GA733 while executing KEU5 and 2970981 - Error message GA733 occur while executing T-code: KEU5.

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture and how to get started
entry.content:
Hi Alicia, I'm glad you found the article insightful. Answering to your question, overcoming resistance to change is one of the most common challenges when introducing Enterprise Architecture (EA) in a business (in reality, introducing any change without proper change management!). 

Since EA often requires shifts in mindset, processes, and the ways different departments collaborate, resistance can stem from various sources—whether it's fear of the unknown, concerns about complexity, or a simple attachment to the status quo.

Some of the strategies that worked for me and can help you:

  • Map your stakeholders and their influence/interests, and engage them since the start, co-creating the vision and making them part of the process. In this same line, identify advocates and build a community. Their enthusiasm and buy-in can help influence others.
  • Define a clear business value, by aligning EA to the business goals and focusing on the tangible benefits. People are more likely to support changes when they can see direct benefits. Try to bring examples, especially those within your industry or with similar challenges.
  • Communicate consistently, transparently and frequently: People resist change when they don't know why it's happening or how it will affect them. Be transparent about the reasons for EA and how it will benefit the organization in both the short and long term. Communication is a 2 ways road, so be open to feedback and consider others' opinions in your iterations.
  • Provide training and support. Offering practical, role-based training can alleviate the fear of the unknown.
  • Focus on quick wins, measure and celebrate the success. In general, incremental changes are more successful, and demonstrating value quickly and celebrating success, helps build momentum and enthusiasm. By measuring progress, you will bring credibility to your initiative.  

Following these, you will be successful not only in implementing EA, but any other initiative. It's all about how you bring people into your journey. Good luck and keep me posted on your success.

entry.title: Re: Completed learning journey to get voucher under Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano

I identify as a person in the below categories:

People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts  Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 

I am eager to learn and upgrade my skills.

entry.title: Re: Sharing Event mesh across subaccounts
entry.content: Hi . Is there a working example ? I am trying to achieve the same but the user provided service is not recognized by the run time.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Q3-Q4 2024 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
Co-Authors: @bilgeakdur @amandamasuku 

Welcome to the future of ESG Reporting and Management with SAP Sustainability Control Tower! This blog post will explore the latest developments in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower and emphasize its crucial role in advancing your Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) agenda.

Positioned at the forefront of sustainability solutions, SAP Sustainability Control Tower is an ERP-centric, cloud-based platform designed to seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other SAP solutions, including SAP Datasphere, SAP EHS Management, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Analytics Cloud. These integration capabilities ensure sustainability is embedded into core business processes, providing automated, auditable, and compliant reporting for ESG data. 

Leveraging AI-driven features, the platform also automates the generation of comprehensive ESG reports and enables businesses to make informed decisions. 

With a strategic focus on expanding regulatory coverage and facilitating integration across solutions, SAP Sustainability Control Tower empowers organizations to achieve their sustainability goals while gaining a competitive advantage in the market.

The following features play a central role in the reporting capabilities solution. Initially, the ESG Data Foundation within the SAP Sustainability Control Tower offers a predefined data model designed to comply with regulatory standards by using pre-built and custom-built sustainability metrics. The solution is transitioning from supporting voluntary reports such as World Economic Forum (WEF) metrics to adopting ESRS and EU Taxonomy, which can also support customers who are starting to address SEC regulations and ISSB standards. Concurrently, this data foundation can also be used for Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) calculation and GHG reporting. 

Stay tuned as we explore the key features and benefits of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower:

  • Carbon Management Features of SAP SCT
  • Generate ESG Reports with AI
  • Disclosure Management Strategy
  • Data Foundation and Extensibility
  • Metric Management
  • Auditability
  • SAP Continuous Influence Program
  • Planned Feature Updates
  • Further Assets

 

Carbon Management Features of SAP SCT

As of June 2024, SCT's corporate carbon management capabilities enable you to accurately calculate both direct and indirect emissions, ensuring compliance and aiding in decarbonization efforts across your operations and supply chains. You can efficiently manage data collection across organizational boundaries and track the completion of collection tasks. 

Our solution allows for the calculation of all categories of corporate emissions, including scope 1, 2, and all sub-categories of scope 3. It supports large-scale calculations based on spending and activities. Additionally, you can leverage an emissions factors library, which includes pre-delivered EPA content. Notably, corporate carbon footprint calculations are a built-in feature of SCT, requiring no additional products or licenses.

With the new Corrective Bookings feature, you can now correct past emissions records within the Emissions DPI. Each correction is fully traceable, displaying the original data alongside the corrected values and the period of correction. This ensures transparency and compliance for auditing purposes.

 

Generate ESG Reports with AI

As of August 2024, you can generate draft reports for periodic internal or external ESG reporting, based on the ESG data available in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower. 

The “Generate ESG Reports with AI” functionality utilizes best-practice templates, generative AI text, and metrics calculated in SAP SCT using the OpenAI GPT 4 model via the SAP AI core service. 

In the first version, users can choose from pre-defined SAP templates. With the planned deliveries in Q4/2024, they will soon be able to edit the templates based on their requirements. Draft reports can be distributed within the organization for reviews, collaboration, and adaptive iterations. 

 

Disclosure Management Strategy

Due to the CSRD disclosure requirements, companies have to also report on how sustainability issues affect their business and the impact of their activities on people and the environment. These requirements are detailed in the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), which require a formal disclosure and exchange of disclosure documents via XBRL format with the authorities.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower aggregates, plans, calculates and steers ESG factors and goals.  Across the world, companies strive to improve their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics and disclose them combined with financial data in their integrated reports. SAP SCT can distribute relevant data into existing disclosure management tools (SAP or non-SAP) to generate integrated reporting. 

Our ambition is to share the ESG metrics available in SAP SCT with disclosure management software to enable the creation, validation, and filing or disclosure of regulatory reports in Word, XBRL, and iXBRL formats, including ESRS. 

Therefore, Thomson Reuters will launch an ESG Disclosure Management app (target availability in Q1/2025), which extends SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SAP SCT) to enable customers with a software solution to simplify their ESG reporting and filing needs. This app will integrate SAP SCT with Thomson Reuters' ONESOURCE Statutory Reporting (TR OSR) to provide customers with last-mile reporting functionality based on the SAP SCT data foundation.

Moreover, the upcoming release of SAP Disclosure Management (target availability in Q4/2024) we release SCT data source connection to seamlessly integrate ESG data between the two solutions to save time and ensure ESG data consistency for existing customers.

 

Data Foundation and Extensibility

As we continue to evolve the SAP Sustainability Control Tower, a key focus is enhancing the extensibility of the ESG Data Foundation. This allows organizations to tailor the platform to their unique sustainability reporting needs, ensuring comprehensive and accurate ESG data management.

We are pleased to introduce a new custom metric type: Standard Custom Metrics. This feature allows you to define calculations directly within the Manage Metrics app and perform them in the Manage ESG Data app, using methods such as sum, division, and weighted division.

With this enhancement, we have updated the names of existing custom metrics to better reflect their functionalities:

  • Basic Metric is now Basic Custom Metric: Create and configure your metric in the Manage Metrics app without requiring measures for the calculation. There is no change in functionality.
  • Advanced Metric is now Legacy Advanced Custom Metric: Metrics and calculation methods previously defined in the Manage Metrics app and calculated in CPE. No new metrics of this type can be created.
  • Standard Custom Metric: Newly introduced, allowing you to define your calculation methodology directly in the Manage Metrics app and calculate in the Manage ESG Data app.

These changes enhance the flexibility and precision of your sustainability metrics, enabling more tailored reporting and analysis.

To streamline your data import processes, you can now import data for Basic Custom Metrics directly using CSV files in the Custom Metrics - Actuals tab of the Manage ESG Data app. Templates are readily available within the app to guide you through the process. This update eliminates the need for the CPE environment for this function, ensuring a more efficient workflow without affecting your existing data.

As of October 2024, we’ve expanded the import and export capabilities to include custom metrics alongside custom measures and dimensions. This functionality is available within the Manage Metrics, Manage Measures, and Manage Custom Dimensions apps. You can now export a set of custom objects and import them into another tenant, facilitating easier data transfer and system setup across different environments. For a comprehensive guide on this process, refer to Exporting and Importing Custom Objects.

To facilitate data import processes, you can now use Microsoft Excel (XLSX/XLS) files in addition to CSV files when importing data in the Manage Master Data and Manage ESG Data apps. The system automatically adapts to typical date formats in Excel files, enhancing ease of use and reducing data formatting issues.

To offer greater customization, dimensions with SAP-provided values now have either Read Only or Active status in the Manage Master Data app. You can add new values to dimensions with Active status, allowing you to seamlessly combine SAP-provided and custom values without creating entirely new custom dimensions.

For example, if the "Contract Type" dimension has four default SAP-provided values and you require an additional value, you can simply add it to the existing dimension. This streamlined approach simplifies dimension management and enhances data consistency.

You can import new master data values via file import or use the new Edit function within the Manage Master Data app.

 

Metric Management

Simplified Creation of Standard Custom Metrics

Defining your calculation methods is now more straightforward. With the Manage Metrics app, you can create Standard Custom Metrics and define your calculation methodology directly within the app, choosing from sum, division, and weighted division methods. This eliminates the need for external calculations and streamlines the metric creation process for many additional metrics ESG standards require.

Assurance Information Configuration

You can now configure the disclosure type, applicable reporting standards, set materiality, and specify the assurance level for each metric in the new Assurance section. This enhancement allows for more detailed reporting and better alignment with regulatory requirements.

 

Auditability

In October, the "View Change Logs" application was released, designed to enhance transparency and compliance with regulatory obligations. This feature allows users to track and review changes across various data categories, including metrics, custom measures, dimensions, and activity data for measures. Users can log all key change types—Create, Update, and Delete—along with old and new values, making it easier to monitor data integrity. This functionality is particularly critical for meeting stringent regulatory requirements. For more information, see  Using the View Change Logs Application.

 

SAP Continuous Influence Program

At SAP, customer feedback is at the heart of our innovation process. With the SAP Continuous Influence program, you have the opportunity to directly impact the future development of SAP products and services. By sharing your ideas, needs, and priorities, you can help shape the direction of our solutions and ensure they meet your business needs.

We are excited to announce the launch of our new SAP Influence page for our Sustainability solutions: SFM (Sustainability Footprint Management), SCT (Sustainability Control Tower), and RDP (Responsible Design and Production). These solutions cover key sustainability topics like ESG-related disclosure, Material Transition, and Carbon Management. This dedicated platform allows you, as a customer or partner, to submit feature requests, vote on others' ideas, and receive timely feedback from SAP.

Check out the session at https://influence.sap.com/go/sustainability and start making your voice heard!

 

Planned Feature Updates

We continue to innovate and expand the capabilities of SAP Sustainability Control Tower. Here are some planned features expected in early Q1:

  • We plan to introduce the ability to create qualitative metrics and collect textual information for selected time periods. This includes support for narrative text and simple questions, allowing you to describe actions and policies. You can assign disclosure references and assurance information in the same way as for quantitative metrics, enhancing the depth of your sustainability reporting.
  •  A new application, Analyze ESG Data to be developed, allowing customers and partners to use advanced filtering capabilities for KPI tiles by time-period and hierarchy. You'll be able to cluster and group KPI tiles by subject matter, frameworks, or any user-defined groups, and personalize custom views. This will help you track the performance of your ESG metrics and group them by strategic priorities.
  • We plan to introduce custom authorization policies that allow you to limit user access to transactional data for specified measures and metrics across various applications. This will enhance data security by preventing unauthorized access to sensitive information, such as financial or HR records.
  • The Manage Metrics app will include new classification fields to better describe the qualities of sustainability metrics. This includes Reporting Standard Version, Disclosure Requirement, and Disclosure References. This improvement will enhance data auditability and compliance alignment.

 

Further Assets

  • Product Feature Videos and Roadmap
  • Customer Stories
  • CSRD Whitepaper

 

Disclaimer:

This blog post, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice.

entry.title: supervisor assignment profile
entry.content:
hello,

 

I would like to select all supervisors of users that i type in, I see this cannot be selected. Is there a way?

entry.title: Share Event mesh between sub account
entry.content:
I have seen same question being replied by other fellow community members , but I don't see any of them marked answered. 

I have 2 sub accounts. A and B. I have event mesh enabled in A , and few apps are deployed in A. Now I have one app , that is deployed in B . It must receive the messages emitted by a service in A. I do not want to manually maintain the credentials of event mesh in credential store and then consume it from the app. I tried creating a UPS but its not resolving the credentials (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/cds-connect#vcap_services , https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sharing-event-mesh-across-subaccounts/qaq-p/12708215 ) . 

Q : Whats the right way to get a user provided service to make the service resolution work ? or is there any other methods to fulfil this, 

 

Sree

entry.title: Re: Multiple bank in bank selection (FPY1 Payment run) depending the contract account
entry.content:
Hi Farid, 

This you have to create the payment medium variant in standard Program SAPFKPY3. 

Then only it will appear here.

Thanks

Amlan

entry.title: Re: Invalid char # replacement
entry.content:
Do you actually require an EBCDIC code page

  • 0120 'EBCDIC 0697/0500 Latin-1, Multilangual'
  • 0160 'EBCDIC character set '7B' pur'

From which system type and how was the binary file created and sent?

entry.title: Re: French new 2025 legal chart of accounts
entry.content:
Hello, could you please advise how we can sort a historical data ( FEC for exmple) if we change just an alternativ account assignment to to initial operation GL Account. As per my understanding Alternativ account value is a dinamic data and once changed, not possible to keep history . 

Thanks for your help, Alexandra

entry.title: Re: how to Get logged in User details in custom SAP FIORI from ECC
entry.content: the logged in user? google, it is answered million times

entry.title: Re: French new 2025 legal chart of accounts
entry.content:
Hi Alexandra,

Alternativ account is stored in ACDOCA during posting.

Within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, report FR_AUDIT_FEC is reading Alternativ account used at the time of the posting, not the one from the Master data. 

In case customer did not maintain correctly its master data at the time of the posting, then solution is reading master data at the time of the FEC creation (but only as exception).

Regards,

Benoit

entry.title: Re: EWM /SCWM/ISU - INITIAL STOCK UPLOAD AND REVERSEL WITH SERIAL NUMBER
entry.content:
Good effort, thanks Omer.

Best Regards

Ozgur

entry.title: Re: latest people profile
entry.content: Hi - for my Firm we have a lot of the 'cards' now on view which we previously controlled through the Employee views section of the RBP. These permissions are no longer respected, rather the views are on for all but the data shows nothing or it looks like an error message when the user selects it. We cant switch this on until we can better configure it all, or until SAP enable the latest people profile to work with the Employee Views options within the RBP setup

entry.title: Re: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD EDITION - CDS_VIEW FOR TAX RATE INFORMATION NOT FOUND

We are facing duplicate material appearing in the planned orders as a result of MRP Live MD01N.
We have run on all materials and only problem occurred in few materials while the master data is same for all.
We tried our best but could not find out the reason for duplicated materials.

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas , script OnEnter with unwanted "enter" f...
entry.content: You could try the command "return true" to exit the script but I'm not sure that will get around the quantity field programming. Have you tried running the script using the onChange event of the material/handling unit field instead of the onEnter event?

entry.title: Re: French new 2025 legal chart of accounts
entry.content:
Thanks a lot for your answer. We are still in ECC, looks like it work differently in S4? Is SAP have any recomendation for ECC users?

With regards, Alexandra

entry.title: Adobe Forms CheckBox on Condition
entry.content:
Dear Devs,

I need a little advice on how to solve this seemingly simple yet tricky topic.

As shown in the image, I need to conditionally control these 3 checkboxes based on a condition.

These checkboxes are grouped inside a subform, which I've bound to my structure VIQMEL.

Objective:

  • If VIQMEL-QMART = 'Z1', then only the first checkbox should be "checked."
  • If VIQMEL-QMART = 'Z2', then only the second checkbox should be "checked."
  • And so on for the last one.

Do you know how this can be managed?

Thank you so much!
Gianni

entry.title: Re: Transition from Custom CDSView to other apps
entry.content:
Hi Soma,

I have not done it before, but have you looked a creating a calculation where you concatenate the necessary fields and generate a URL to the desired app?

 

 

There also might be a better option looking into KPIs and Reports or some dashboard with one of the other analytics features.

Check out SP6 - there is a good guide there to walk you through several examples

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-058/2408/SolP/2P6?region=DE

I hope this helps

Thank you
Jerry

entry.title: Re: How to include Qty of material loss happens during Pa...

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024 - VRP-13823 - Refresh Employee History by Emp...
entry.content:
@InesTorres 

I think you missed the accepted solution message : https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/2h-2024-vrp-13823-refresh-employee-history-by-employee/qaq-p/13926214/comment-id/38557#M38557

You need to use "By Last Run Date" to do what you need, you don't need to do a full EC import. This enhancement was made for a specific type of configuration that you most likely don't have in your template.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Displaying dunning notices
Re: Integration of ABAP world in SAP Build
entry.content: What about the 2 build project lobby features  Managing Project Members and Transport a Project ? Is it available for ABAP projects ?

entry.title: Re: French new 2025 legal chart of accounts
entry.content:
Hi Alexandra,

Yes, behavior is different between ECC and S/4HANA.

We do not have a SAP recommandation in that ECC case.

As a consulting activities it could be proposed to change alternative account assignment when FEC is produced (but risk needs to be assessed).

Please note also the following about 2024/2025 Financial Statements : Expected display is described at the very end of the documentation (FR). : https://www.anc.gouv.fr/files/anc/files/1_Normes_fran%C3%A7aises/Reglements/2022/R2022_06/R2022_06_version-commentee.pdf

Regards,

Benoit

I

entry.title: A Deep Dive into Role-Based Permissions: Best Practices for Access Management
entry.content:
What are Role-based Permissions?

Role-based permissions are a security model in SAP SuccessFactors that allows you to grant or restrict the system access to any user.

It helps organizations ensure that the right people are getting the right permissions in the system.

Role based permissions has three main elements called:-

  • Permission Groups
  • Permission Roles
  • Target Population
  1. Permission Groups – Group of Employees

Permission groups are the user or user’s who share similar permissions or certain attributes.

  1. Permission Roles – Set of permissions

Permission roles are set of permissions a user can access or do in the system.

  1. Target Population – Target population are employees for whom behalf the action has been performed. It is denoted by t.

Note:- All permissions doesn’t required the target population, only some permissions requires that and it’s denoted by t at the end of the permission.

 

You can identify the permission groups, roles and target population by finding out who, What and whom in your requirements. Where Who represents the permission groups, what represents the permission roles and whom respresents the target population.

 

To simplify the steps, you can divide it into below steps:-

  1. Permission Groups
  2. Permission Roles
  3. Granted Population
  4. Targeted Population
  5. Assign permissions to the Granted Population
  6. Define who are the targeted Population

Process to implement the RBP is as below:-

Permission Groups -> Permission Roles -> Granting Permission Roles to Permission Groups - > Target Population

Let’s try to understand the role-based permissions with example. Suppose a customer wants that only HR managers can create/update or delete the objectives/goals for employees in US.

 

Here HR manager in USA will be our Granted population.

Create/Update or delete the goals and other permissions are permission roles.

All employees who work in USA are the targeted population.

 

Procedure to Implement RBP

Pre-requisites-

You must have access to role based permission.

 

  1. Permission Groups

To create Permission Groups, User must have relevant Permission.

In Demo Environment, I logged in as Aanya Singh(sfadmin){Superadmin for the system}

Procedure:-

In Homepage, under search for actions or people search for Manage Permission Groups -> Create New - > Give a unique name -> Select the employees you want to Grouped -> Done.

 

Give a Name to your Group Name-> Select the employee whom you want to give permission.

In below example:-  We have grouped Human resource employees who are working in US and excluded the employees who job level is Executive Support.

Group Name :: Granted_User_Sugandha

User Type :: Employee

 

 

Result:-

Now we have successfully created the group Granted_user_Sugandha under permission groups, you can see we have 23 Active memberships.

 

 

  1. Permission Roles

To create Permission Groups, User must have relevant Permission.

In Demo Environment, I logged in as Aanya Singh(sfadmin){Superadmin for the system}

Procedure:-

Go to Manage Permission Roles -> Create - > Basic information -> Add Permission -> Done.

 

Img 2.1                                                                              Img 2.1

 

The next step is to fill in Basic information in Create Role such as Name and description(optional). In the example below, we have named Human Resource_US (Sugandha_Test) -> Click Next.

                                                                           Img2.2

Add Permissions, Give the required permission you want to give to the user. We have  User Permission and Administrative Permission.

After adding the required permission, save it.

 

  1. Granting Permission Roles to Groups

Go to Permission roles -> Under Human Resource_US_Test -> Add Role Assignment -> Basic Information - > 

 

 Whom you want to give access, to all employees or from Groups, If Group than Which group you want to give access, select the group & if you want to add their direct managers you can do and Save.

 

  1. Target Population

Note- We have given additional permission in the roles for human resource_US as shown in below image, Under User permissions, Object plan permission need a Target Population (Denoted by t symbol), Where we need to add Target population. In below example Human resource_US can create a goal for employees in US., our target population is employees in USA.

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Management installation on SAP HANA as...
entry.content:
Hi @Ryno_Abrie_MCG 

Disclosure Management itself will only run on SQL Server (i.e. for its own tables, like Report.Chapters, Security.Users). It will not run on any other DB platform

You can define data-sources to S4HANA, BW, ERP etc.

Marc

entry.title: SVT PROD Scenario
entry.content:
Hello,

I have already created two threads for the same issue earlier and do not want to mix the topic again hence starting new. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/substance-volume-tracking-production-scenario-and-bom/qaq-p/13625263

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/svt-production-scenario/qaq-p/13683504

There is still a gap in understanding SVT Production scenario. Frankly admitting the logic behind it is not clear. Even the SVT master note with example does not help to me.

I have one example:

A Production Order is created for Main material (M1), it has a single component(M2) and a BOM with other material (L1,L2,L3 etc).  
Main material M1 is assigned to a Real sub R1
Component M2 is assigned to Real sub R2. 
Real sub R1 & R2 both has composition with 9 substance which needs to be track.e.g C1,C2,C3 etc.

See attached Pic- High level Idea


Issue:
By maintaining 'SVT relevant Usage' for Real sub R1(assigned to Material A1), all 9 components show up in Planned table. As soon as I maintain 'SVT Relevant usage' for second level Real sub R2 (which is assigned to component M2), only 2 components are shown in Planned qty table. Rest 7 are not showing. (CCRCT_PRPL). FILL & VT program is executed after every change so as to update SVT table.

TECO is not yet done.

  • All components are available in EHS COMP table and few in REG table
  • We do not use material relevant classification.
  • Customization for 'Selection criteria' is correct, else components would not have shown.
  • 'SVT relevant' usage means, usage that is exclusively created for SVT tracking.

Question

Not clear what is causing other component to disappear as soon as the SVT Relevant usage is maintained for down the level substances.
What is the high-level logic used for SAP Production scenario?

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2200
entry.content:
Hi @peterhaas77 

 

Stack 2200 has been released

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay Eligibility Fields in Design Worksheet
entry.content:
Hi @JoannaRo 

In VarPay these fields allow us to replace the "Manager Eligibility Rules" which is the exact same thing than the regular Eligibility Rules in Comp that allow us to include or exclude someone based on UserID. See the design with these Eligibility fields that can be made in Comp (not Varpay) to save time when running mass update job and remove the need to click on "Apply" the rules before launching worksheets.

 

That being said this type of exclusion doesn't make much sense in Varpay and I've personnally never used them in any of my implementations.

Why ? because we already have all we need in Plan Setup > Settings > Employee Central Settings with the following two options: 

 

The Global Eligibility Rule above does the exact same thing than the Manager Eligibility rule (except it always includes - we cannot switch it around like Manager Eligiblity rule to exclude) AND adds the possibility to define a date that would be different from the program end date or the template EC effective date which the Manager Eligibility rule doesn't allow (it always uses the EC effective date).

And the Eligibility Rule for Employee History Records allows us to fine tune which records we want. And if no record is retrieved for someone then that person will never be added to the worksheet.

In summary, these eligibility fields could work but there is no need for them technically.

Hope this helps you understand better

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Transaction Massive for Print Production Order
entry.content:
Hi gg8,

How are you outputting them today? Isn't the output usually generated on Create?

There are several Create apps that allow you to mass create the orders - there is also the mass processing app

 

For your second question, please see the help:

Printing Order Attachments Automatically 

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: "SAP Integration Suite application is not subscribed"...
entry.content:
A workaround to this is assigning the roles to your user account manually and instantiating an instance of the SAP Process Integration Runtime service.... 

  1. Once the capabilities are active, assign the roles in the screenshot.
  2. Assign the SAP Process Integration Runtime entitlement to the subaccount.
  3. Create a new instance of SAP Process Integration Runtime
  4. Create a service key in the SAP Process Integration Runtime instance. It will provide you the credentials required.

entry.title: Re: API Proxy 401 Unauthorized
entry.content: how the authentication method is setup on api/btp ? if its default to S user, you need to link the S user to Basis3.

entry.title: Re: Workflow Email Notification not triggered for condition contract

entry.title: Re: Net value is 0 in settlement document SD
entry.content:
Hi @kutaysarigul, 


You can refer to the SAP standard configuration guide in KBA 2535889 for further details:
https://me.sap.com/notes/2535889/E

You can also specifically review your condition types and pricing procedure.

Regards, 
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: Re: Passing Bas64 Encoded string of a PDF as Email attachment from Process Automation
entry.content: Thanks Dan for the response. Attachment will be a part of an automation. It will be passed inside an approval form.

entry.title: Inbound Delivery line item stock type not updating as quality stock
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a scenario where the storage location used in the PO is linked to External Warehouse and when the inbound delivery is created the stock type at the item level is coming as blank which is leading to no creation of inspection lot when the material document is posted through EDI from the external system. 

Can someone explain does the plant-storage location-warehouse assignment will have an impact on the stock type determination at the inbound delivery line item level? If I use the same data except the different storage location which is not linked to warehouse we have no issues. 

Appreciate if someone explain if there are any additional configurations to be maintained to activate the same in order to generate the inspection lot when material document idoc is posted from the external system to ECC. 

We are connecting SAP ECC system to External 3PL warehouse provider.

entry.title: Re: How to add new value in Responsibility Definitions?
entry.content: Does anyone know about this configuration? its related with condition contract management workflow.

entry.title: Re: How to write a Groovy Script For Multilable, and what...
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please check the following code:

import com.sap.it.api.mapping.Output

def void multilabel(int[] var1, Output output) {
    if (var1.length > 0) {
        int b = var1[0]
        for (int i = 0; i < b; i++) {
            output.addValue(i + 1)
        }
    }
}

BR,

Andrzej

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Access Denied
entry.content: Thanks for sharing Dan. I used this video as reference while I was setting up my Trail account. Still, I am getting Access Denied

entry.title: Re: Automatically assess your migration from SAP Process Orchestration Systems to SAP Integration Su
entry.content:
Hi Richard,

can Migration Assessment estimate message metric based on Process Orchestration monitor?

Thanks in advance

Nicola

entry.title: Time Tracking - Absence calendar; workflow request boxes;
entry.content:
Dear community,

I am currently supporting my colleagues in Vietnam with the implementation of their new time tracking module (a plant with ~300 employees working in 3 shifts). During this time, we have encountered some issues that I would like to share with the community. If anyone has any ideas or recommendations on how we can proceed - we would be very happy!

1) Team absence calendar

Is there any way to customise the team absence calendar section, for example, my colleagues would like to see the overview match their payroll cycle which is from the 23rd to the 22nd of each month (instead of the standard view from 1st to the 31st of each month).

2) Workflow requests

Currently we do not have a solution to show more information/details of the request in the individual request fields (in the workflow request overview). This way it is very difficult to review the requests in advance (e.g. as HR) as they have to click on each request to get more details.

3) Deduction of break on non planned working days

Sometimes employees have to work on Sundays. When their time is updated (via the interface), the system calculates the actual working time, but if that day was irregular, there is now a predefined break. So HR needs to make sure that the times are adjusted so that the break is deducted before the times are taken for payroll. Is there a way to improve this in the system?

These are some of the issues we are currently facing. It would be great if someone could check our points and give us feedback. Thanks in advance!!!

Best regards

Sabrina

entry.title: Re: Error Info... V2 101: Error during update of Table VBUP
entry.content: Thank you really much for sharing your solution.

entry.title: Re: Copy a Tenant Database using system replication
entry.content:
Hello Andreas, 

Why did you mention restarting both databases before starting the replication? Are the parameter changes made in the source database not applied online?

Additionally, what are the advantages of this method compared to a backup/restore approach?

I only need to copy a tenant database for a new SAP build and was considering if there is a feature similar to Oracle RMAN duplicate.



BR, Yurii

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Access Denied
entry.content: Thanks, abhashsinha. Custom IAS user have BuildApps_Administrator and BuildApps_Developer role collections. Yesterday, It showed me "Access Denied". Now it's working. Thanks a lot

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Access Denied
entry.content: Hey Dan! It's working now. Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content: Regarding the question from 17 Okt 2024: Currently it is not possible in Resource Scheduling (RSH) to see the utilisation of individuals, without each having their own work centre. The responsible product manager told me today that in private(!) cloud we plan to bring the person planning topic back to the roadmap.

entry.title: Re: Adobe Forms CheckBox on Condition
entry.content:
Hi Gianni,

Create a hidden field named QMART that is bound to VIQMEL-QMART inside subform BOX_GREY.  Set the checkboxes so they are unbound and calculated, and use the following Javascript for each field, and of course the string value would change for each checkbox.

this.rawValue = this.parent.QMART.rawValue == "Z1" ? 1 : 0;

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: In DataPaging SAP MDK, paging is not showing new data.
entry.content: Can you please confirm if the list is an Object Table or Object Collection?

entry.title: Re: Sybase Mainframe Connect (Mainframe Connect component: OpenServer Connect for CICS compatibility
entry.content: z/OS 3.1 is already listed in PAM as a compatible OS. Please check it here: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/pam?hash=s%3DSYBASE%2520MAINFRAME%2520CONNECT%252015.0%26filter%3D%26o%3Dmost_viewed%257Cdesc%26st%3Dl%26rpp%3D20%26page%3D1%26pvnr%3D67838200100900007484%26pt%3Dt%257COS%26ainstnr%3D67838200104900019984%26fclfilter%3DG1%257CZ%252FOS

entry.title: Re: Binding transfer problem
entry.content: Yes you can share the full project in a support case

entry.title: Re: How to replicate data from Sybase ASE to MSSQL
entry.content: Hi, assuming you are not using ASE as a Business Suite database, then you would normally use ASE to Replication Server to EnterpriseConnect Data Access (ECDA) to MSSQL. However, ECDA is End of Sale, so you likely cannot purchase it.

entry.title: Re: Assets - Calculation Methods / Period Control Methods
entry.content: Hello, Thank you for your question. I've forwarded it to the experts.

entry.title: Re: Is Sybase Mainframe Connect v15 compatible with z16?
entry.content: Hi, I'm not sure what you mean by z16, since the mainframe OS is currently 2.x and 3.1. You can find compatibility information in PAM: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/pam?hash=s%3DSYBASE%2520MAINFRAME%2520CONNECT%252015.0%26filter%3D%26o%3Dmost_viewed%257Cdesc%26st%3Dl%26rpp%3D20%26page%3D1%26pvnr%3D67838200100900007484%26pt%3Dt%257COS%26ainstnr%3D67838200104900019984%26fclfilter%3DG1%257CZ%252FOS.

entry.title: Re: BADI to update partner profit center while creating j...
entry.content: Yes, I did try but it doesn't have field Partner profit center in changing parameters

entry.title: Read Rate plan (with pricing scheme) data / charges via API
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need to read data of rate plans set up in our Subscription billing system -  the most important information i need to retrieve is the charge(s) assigned to the rate plans.
I tested following services both from the Try out page of the Acclerator Hub, and via Postman:

- Basic Rate Plan Functions :
/rate-plans (GET)
/rate-plans{ratePlanId} (GET)

- Rate Plan View
/rate-plans/{ratePlanId}/view (GET)
/rate-plans/view/$batch (request = get in the body)

In all the cases, the only data retrieved is the following:

"id": "019179ef-2fad-43f8-bb6b-e5ec81f00555",
    "description": "Abonnement non forfaitaire, 4 rate elements, no subelements",
    "periodicity": "calendar-monthly",
    "endOfMonthRule": "billFromCurrentCycle",
    "currency": "EUR",
    "billingProfileId": "_initial",
    "snapshots": [
        {}
    ],
    "marketId": "BE00"
 
How can I get the full set of data?
In the response of tests executed in the Accelerator hub Sandbox, only rate plans  that do NOT refer to pricing schemes are retrieved.
On the other hand, all our rate plans DO refer to pricing schemes.
Is this the reason why only incomplete data are retrieved? If so, what's the solution?

Thank you in advance for your help.
 
Kind Regards,
Elena

entry.title: Re: In DataPaging SAP MDK, paging is not showing new data.
entry.content:
@bill_froelich It's an Object Table

"_Type": "Control.Type.SectionedTable",
"_Name": "SectionedTable0",

entry.title: Re: Cost Monitoring for Direct Capitalization using Statistical Project in s/4 hana public cloud.
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question: it has been forwarded to the experts. Please help us to answer your question more quickly by using the right tag: for Finance-related questions, it's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance. Here are some more best practices: Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/q-amp-a-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-how-to-get-your-answer-faster/ba-p/13934481). Kind regards, Henrike)

entry.title: Professionelles Change Management mit dem SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer
entry.content:
Die Einführung einer SAP Cloud-Lösung ist mehr als ein reines IT-Projekt, sie ist eine ganzheitliche Businesstransformation. Was heißt das konkret? Unternehmen profitieren von neuen Best Practice-Prozessen, der Automatisierung von Routineaufgaben, einer schnellen Verfügbarkeit von Daten und einer hohen Transparenz von Informationen. Für die betroffenen Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeiter bedeutet das, sich von liebgewonnenen Gewohnheiten zu verabschieden und eine neue Art der Zusammenarbeit zu leben. Dieser Prozess läuft umso reibungsloser, je besser die Belegschaft dabei unterstützt, informiert und auf die neue Situation vorbereitet und qualifiziert wird.

Der Mehrwert eines projektbegleitenden Change Managements ist deshalb unstrittig. Aber welche konkreten Aktivitäten sind zu welchem Zeitpunkt notwendig, um die sogenannte “softe” Seite einer Cloud-Einführung professionell abzudecken? Und was ist bei der Planung und Umsetzung von Maßnahmen zu beachten?

Für alle Projekte, die der bewährten SAP-Activate Implementierungsmethodologie folgen, bietet der Roadmap Viewer von SAP praktische Antworten auf diese Fragen. Dort ist das Thema „Organizational Change Management“ seit kurzem als Querschnittsthema integriert. Interessierte finden eine kompakte Darstellung des Change Management-Ansatzes der SAP mit seinen sechs Dimensionen und den zugehörigen Arbeitspaketen (sogenannte „Deliverables“) für die einzelnen Projektphasen. Die folgende Abbildung zeigt die Deliverables, sortiert nach Activate-Phasen, im Überblick:

Alle Deliverables werden detailliert beschrieben: Eine einführende Erläuterung gibt einen Überblick, und klar strukturierte Umsetzungsschritte zeigen auf, wie die Implementierung in der Projektpraxis erfolgt. Zusätzliche Unterstützung bieten praktische Tools und Templates, die heruntergeladen und auf den jeweiligen Projektkontext angepasst werden können.

Interessiert? Alle Informationen zu Change Management finden Sie kostenlos unter diesem Link. Für den Zugriff auf den Roadmap Viewer ist lediglich eine Registrierung (Universal ID für SAP-Inhalte) erforderlich. Viel Erfolg bei der Change Management-Begleitung Ihrer Cloud-Implementierung!

Und noch ein Hinweis: Wenn Sie sich für weitere Angebote rund um das Thema Change Management interessieren, besuchen Sie unsere Web-Site Organizational Change Management mit SAP.

entry.title: BTP service plan "Process Integration Runtime" and Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I need to understand a question: I need to know if it is possible to use Service Instance and Service Key for inbound authentication for i-flow of Integration suite (basic edition) by enabling "Process Integration Runtime". To enable "Process Integration Runtime", we need to enable "Cloud Foundry" in our subaccount. I would like to know if there are any costs associated with enabling "Cloud Foundry" in the subaccount. If there are, how much does it cost?

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Professionelles Change Management mit dem SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer
entry.content: Danke Doris für den Hinweis auf den neuen Bereich für Organizational Change Management als Teil von SAP Activate.

entry.title: Can we add both Data structure and table to Sq01 Query?
entry.content:
I would like to add one structure and one table to the same query using Sq01.

These structure doesn`t have any table. So what should I do? Should I create the tables first and then add the queries or is there any other way?

entry.title: Re: Unable to delete the released work order
entry.content:
Hi,

The deletion flag cannot be set for a work order with release status.

You can first set the status of the work order to TECO->CLSD by first technically completing and then business completing the work order.

If this works for a single order in your case, then you can set the work orders to TECO and CLSD in mass using standard report/transactions.

Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: Re: The customer and supplier banks check the validation of the configuration point
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question: it has been forwarded to the experts. Please help us to answer your question more quickly by using the right tag: for Finance-related questions, it's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance. Here are some more best practices: Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/q-amp-a-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud...). Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Error - Actual Assessment is replaced by Universal Allocation
entry.content:
When trying to access the App - 'Create Overhead Allocation Cycles - Cost Centers - Actual' (KSU1) the following error is returned - 

Actual Assessment is replaced by Universal Allocation. See text.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error - Actual Assessment is replaced by Universal Al...
entry.content:
This is detailed in the KBA - 

3486075 - Deprecation of Apps "Overhead Allocation Cycles - Cost Centers – Actual"(KSU*) - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Cause

The information about deprecation means the above apps (KSU*) still work and are officially supported, but it is not recommended to use them anymore. No improvements / new features can be expected. 

Resolution

The above apps (KSU*) are blocked for new customers, which have not been using the apps previously. All existing customers which have been using the apps, will be able to continue using the apps. 

The functionality of this app will be replaced by Universal Allocation. If you are not yet using this app productively, consider switching to the Universal Allocation apps.

entry.title: Is it possible to perform GR for Purchase order automatically (mouvement 101) in sap public cloud ?
entry.content: Is it possible to perform GR for Purchase order automatically (mouvement 101) in sap public cloud ?

entry.title: Re: RAP Unmanaged Scenario Example Which Should Include CRUD Operations, and Actions
entry.content: Nice Blog. Can the ADT part be done from BAS in the cloud. Or do you need a local version of Eclipse IDE installed.

entry.title: Re: How can the documents listed in the report 'RFDOPR20'...
entry.content: Dear Raymond Giuseppi, I am analyzing a single debtor. In their master data, the checkbox 'Payment history record' is activated. However, I am unable to filter the corresponding 'Incoming payments during the period' used from SAP system to calculate the 'Average arrears with cash discount payments' in report RFDOPR20. I am utilizing the following fields from the BSEG table to identify the relevant 'Incoming payments.

entry.title: Re: How can the documents listed in the report 'RFDOPR20'...
entry.content: BSEG-BUKRS = Company Code BSEG-GJAHR = 2024 BSEG-AUGDT = 01.11.2024 until 30.11.2024 BSEG-AUGBL <> Space BSEG-KOART = D BSEG-KUNNR = Customer

entry.title: Re: How can the documents listed in the report 'RFDOPR20'...
entry.content: BSEG-BSCHL = Posting keys relevant for incoming payments

entry.title: Re: SAP BI Server tuning
entry.content: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/performance-tuning/role/web-server/tuning-iis-10

entry.title: 401k/Roth combined employer match issue
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have an issue when we match a  combined contribution of a 401(k) and a Roth plan

401(k) and Roth = 100% match on the first 3% and then 50% match on the next 3% which equates to a MAX of 4.50% employer match.

Scenario:

Employee elects the two at:

3% 401(k) = 3% Employer Match

3% Roth = 3% Employer Match

SAP calculates a total of 6% employer match which exceeds the 4.50% employer match.

Has anyone else experienced this and if so, what have you done to resolve this?

entry.title: Possibility of setting up new document type for returns checkbox to be enabled for STO process
entry.content:
We are trying to set up a returns Process for STO , so wanted to know if we can enable the returns checkbox / field for return STO cases.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Possibility of setting up new document type for retur...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Kindly refer to the kBA:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2903987/E

 

Best Regards

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: MB5B negative opening period stock altough no material document
entry.content:
Hi, 

When I checked MB5B between following period there is negative opening stock.

But this material hasn't any movement in those perids. There is no material document from 01.01.2000 to 31.01.2024

The batch tracking has been active for this material since 20.01.2024. Could the negative opening stock be related to the selection parameters on the MB5B screen?

entry.title: Re: Titular/NombreRazon empty in SII XML for Spain
entry.content: Hello, thank you very much for your question; it has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Merge dimension using left of string
entry.content:
HI,

Is it possible to merge dimensions using a substring of a dimension?

Ex. I have one query with order numbers and in the second query I have the same order number but followed by an underscore. How do I merge the data?

Query 1

[Order Number]

Query 2

[Order Number_Order Type]

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Extensibility – Guide to extend API_BUSINESS_PARTNER API for Standard Fields
entry.content: do you really have to add that association?

entry.title: Re: Shortened fiscal year
entry.content: Hello, Thank you for your question. I've forwarded it to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Get ready to discover hidden opportunities with Community Demand Trends on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
What if you could watch market trends and fluctuations in demand as they happen? On January 1st, 2025, all Platinum suppliers on SAP Business Network will have this capability, with the release of Community Demand Trends. 

Community Demand Trends is a powerful tool for any supplying organization, allowing them to react fast to any change and identify previously hidden sales opportunities by spotting trends and variations in demand in near-real-time fashion. This provides the insights and analysis they need to make fact-based strategic decisions in a timely manner and keep their competitiveness as suppliers.

A new way to leverage SAP Business Network
Evolving way beyond being a simple transactional platform, SAP Business Network will offer commerce data worth 5 trillion $ to the lens of the suppliers. Channel manager or sales individual will use Demand Trends to evaluate buying behaviors of their own customer base, explore demand trends across the entire network (e.g. the top growing commodities), and check their performance relative to all transactions of trading partners on SAP Business Network.

A unique capability in many ways
Community Demand Trends is unique in width, with suppliers accessing an ecosystem representing more than 5 trillion US$ of spend. The data consists of anonymized PO data and is enriched by machine learning.
It is also unique in its granularity, offering filters at the commodity level. Suppliers can explore demand by UNSPSC segment, family, and class, as well as filter by parent or child account, customer, region or time range.

Here's how to get ready
Here are a few things you can do prior to January 1st, 2025, to ensure that you be able to use Community Demand Trends:

  • Check that your account is at the Platinum subscription level,
  • Inform sales managers, channel managers, analysts and other colleagues from your organization that might benefit from these unique insights,
  • As an account administrator, create relevant users and ensure they have the permission "Analytics Dashboard Access" enabled.

Once released, you'll be able to access Demand Trends from the Reports tab. 

Here's to a successful year 2025!

entry.title: Re: usage of cds.foreach in typescript
entry.content:
The solution:

 

cds.foreach(cds.parse.cql("SELECT ID FROM db_table"), (each: table) => console.log(each.ID))

 

or pass a CQN object. Locks are currently not supported, see https://github.com/cap-js/cds-types/issues/331 & https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/335478/

entry.title: Re: Live Data Connectivity to Google Big Query
entry.content: Anything new about this? Do you know if it has been postponed?

entry.title: HTML5 App published but not showing in Work Zone Content Manager
entry.content:
Hi,

I followed the following tutorial on the trial account to publish my Incident Management App created using Business Application Studio, while testing the CAP development, as made from the previous tutorials of this one:

Deploy to CF 

Everything went as expected, my cf apps command in then BAS  Terminal returning the 2 apps, as in the screenshot of the tutorial, and my cf services  command returns 4 services, because HANA Cloud has been moved from space to subaccount section.

 

However, I still do not see my app within the Content Manager of Work Zone, as mentioned in the following tutorial:

Integrate with Work Zone 

 

What could be the reason?

 

Following are the screenshots of my Role Collections, Subaccount Instances, CF Space and Work Zone Content Manager.

 

Thanks in advance.
